stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Hiroshi Sugimoto’s photograph Seascape: Bay of Sagami (1990) shows a horizon dividing sea and sky in long exposure.
  • The image uses extended exposure times to convey a sense of timeless stillness across the natural world.
  • His photograph Lake Superior, Eagle River (2003) shows another horizon between water and sky captured under similar conditions.
  • This image also relies on long exposures and minimal compositional elements.
  • Both works are intended to evoke contemplative stillness through the unchanging horizon line.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two photographs. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Seascape: Bay of Sagami and Lake Superior, Eagle River both rely on long exposures of a horizon to evoke contemplative stillness.
Sugimoto’s Lake Superior, Eagle River uses minimal compositional elements to convey timeless stillness across the natural world.
Sugimoto completed Seascape: Bay of Sagami in 1990 and Lake Superior, Eagle River in 2003.
While Seascape: Bay of Sagami depicts an oceanic horizon, Lake Superior, Eagle River depicts a freshwater horizon.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. It names both photographs and asserts a shared feature (long exposures of a horizon to evoke contemplative stillness), drawn from notes that overlap on both works.

Choice B is incorrect because it describes only one photograph (one-sided foil). Choice C is incorrect because it states a difference in completion years rather than a similarity. Choice D is incorrect because it emphasizes a difference between the subjects.
